transactions in the books of accounts, and the elimination of reconciled
balance of the reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other
Funds, in the consolidated Statement of Financial Position at yearend.

2.7 We recommended and it was agreed that management ensure the following:
a) The property unit and accounting office conduct reconciliation of the
RPCPPE and PPELCs or the PPE Subsidiary Ledgers and any differences
be investigated/verified and appropriate adjustments be made on both
records for reconciling items noted;
b) The Inventory Committee make a physical inventory of all the property of
the Municipality, including land improvements, infrastructure assets;
building and other structures, and furniture and fixtures; and
c) The property unit and accounting office follow the proper numbering
system in assigning property numbers for the PPEs as required under COA
Circular 2020-006, and ensure that the property number assigned to each
PPE is the same in both the RPCPPE and PPELCs.

unrecorded PPEs and drop from the books of accounts those turned-over and
under the control/possession of other government agencies with the following
proposed journal entries:
Motor Vehicles 2,421,500.00
Information and Communication Technology 576,538.00
Equipment
Machinery 217,000.00
Medical Equipment 617,640.00
Sports Equipment 120,170.00
Government Equity 3,952,848.00
To take up the unrecorded PPEs found at station during physical count.
Accumulated Depreciation-Other Land Improvement 5,746.47
Accumulated Depreciation-Building and Other 206,215.61
Structures
Prior Years Adjustment 5,857,121.68
Other Land Improvement 383,097.57
Building and Other Structures 5,685,986.19
To transfer and drop from the books of accounts PPEs turned-over to and
under the control/possession of other government agencies.

4.5 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a) Draw the following proposed journal entries to drop from the books of
accounts the PPE items below the threshold:
Accumulated Depreciation 54,653.34
Prior Years Adjustment 449,277.78
Information and Communication
Technology Equipment 521,660.00
To drop from the books of accounts the PPE items below the threshold.
b) Henceforth, ensure that only tangible assets which meet the ₱50,000.00
threshold be recorded as PPE, and those below the threshold be recorded
either as Inventory-Semi-Expendable Property or Expense if issued to the
end-users.

5.5 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a) Draw the appropriate accounting entries to adjust or reverse the
depreciation of PPE items that were dropped from the books of accounts;
and
b) Review the journal entries that resulted in negative PPE balances and
reduction in value of a PPE account more than its recorded cost, and draw
the appropriate adjusting journal entries to correct the deficiencies.

6.7 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer
agreed to:
a) Cause the actual cash transfer of the unexpended LDRRMF-QRF
and MOOE from the GF to TF books of accounts with the following
accounting journal entries:
GF Books (LDRRMF):
Due to Other Funds 3,730,466.15
Cash in Bank 3,730,466.15
To record transfer of Unspent QRF and MOOE to TF books.
TF Books:
Cash in Bank 3,730,466.15
Due from Other Funds 3,730,466.15
To record receipt of unspent QRF and MOOE from GF.
b) Henceforth, ensure actual cash transfer of the unexpended LDRRMF-QRF
and MOOE to the TF books of accounts at yearend.

7.5 We recommended and management agreed to revert back the unexpended
LDRRMF for more than five years totaling ₱425,488.67 to the unappropriated
surplus of the GF and re-appropriate the same to other social
projects/programs through the Sangguniang Bayan (SB).

8.6 We recommended and it was agreed that management enforce the
following:
a) The Municipal Engineer submit any additional suspension and resumption
orders issued for proper evaluation, otherwise, require the respective
contractors to refund/repay the overpayments due to understatements of
LD totaling ₱345,861.27;
b) Require the contractor concerned to refund/repay the overpayment due to
erroneous computation of work accomplished amounting to ₱25,708.79;
c) Require the contractors concerned to rectify the damaged sections of the
Kiangan Public Market Continuation/Improvement project amounting to
₱68,326.59, and to install the receptacles and incandescent bulbs worth
₱1,107.15 of the Kiangan Public Market Building;
d) Compute the amount of LD for the 19 days delay in the completion of the
Kiangan Public Market Building and require the contractor to refund the
same; and
e) Henceforth, ensure the proper determination of actual work accomplished,
correct computation and imposition of the LD, and the immediate
rectification of project deficiencies by the contractors concerned.

9..6 We recommended and management agreed to appropriate funds for the
₱102,009.00 deficiency of the 10% LGU Equity in order to ensure payment of
the contract for the road project once completed and to avoid the incurrence
of irregular expenditure.

10.8 We recommended and management agreed to:
a) Stop immediately the grant of TA to Municipal Officials concerned;
b) Submit written explanations/justifications on the grant of TA to Municipal
Officials who were issued/assigned and/or had been using government
vehicles and why it should not be disallowed in audit; and
c) Henceforth, ensure compliance with the pertinent laws and regulations on
the grant of TA to its officials in order to avoid unlawful expenditures and
audit disallowances.

11.3 We commended Management for the withholding and prompt remittance of
taxes and contributions/premiums for Philhealth, Pag-ibig and GSIS and
recommended for their continuous compliance hereof.

office of the Municipal Engineer to advertise the procurement of

projects) to entice licensed contractors to participate in the bidding;

free from RROW problems, and donated lots are secured and
processed in the name of the Municipality to avoid problems during
project implementation;

Municipal Engineer to revisit all unimplemented projects to

Council for the reversion thereof;

implemented 20% Development Fund Projects and recommend to
the LCE for its reversion to un-appropriated surplus for re-
appropriation to other priority development projects; and

include in the succeeding annual/supplemental budget the reverted
funds from unimplemented projects and savings from implemented
projects to fund other priority development projects and services.
